skill.name: elevenlabs-music-generation
skill.description: Generate songs, jingles, loops, and instrumental beds with ElevenLabs Music through the RunComfy CLI. The skill helps users structure style prompts, lyrics, duration, vocal settings, and output formats for the hosted model.
skill.summary: Create structured ElevenLabs Music tracks through the RunComfy CLI.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command, URL, filesystem, and weak-cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. Most matches are markdown examples or substring false positives. Real risk comes from scoped RunComfy CLI execution, hosted API calls, and local token storage.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution With Hosted Model Calls
medium_findings.description: The skill can run runcomfy commands and call the ElevenLabs Music model. This is expected behavior, but it creates execution and network risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowlist and examples directly show runcomfy execution and API submission. This is legitimate for the skill, but still a risk factor.

medium_findings.title: Local API Token Storage Requirement
medium_findings.description: The documentation says runcomfy login writes an API token to a hidden home configuration file. Users must protect that local credential.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and permissions are stated directly in SKILL.md. This is normal CLI credential storage, not malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs Are Declared References
low_findings.description: The detected URLs point to RunComfy documentation, model pages, and stated outbound endpoints. No unrelated callback endpoint was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains relate to RunComfy services and documentation. No hidden data transfer was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography matches occur in prose and field names. No cryptographic function, cipher selection, or hashing implementation appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no crypto API or algorithm usage at the flagged lines. The signal appears to be substring matching.

low_findings.title: Shell Metacharacter Mentions Are Defensive Documentation
low_findings.description: The command-substitution alert appears in a security note about shell metacharacters. It is not an executable user-controlled command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is explanatory prose about shell injection. No command at this line expands user prompt content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard Bash Allowlist For RunComfy
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits Bash(runcomfy *) commands. This is scoped, but it requires trust in the installed RunComfy CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard allowlist is explicit and affects runtime behavior. Its scope is narrower than arbitrary Bash.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt Data Sent To External Model API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill sends music prompts and lyrics to RunComfy-hosted ElevenLabs Music infrastructure. Users should avoid secrets or sensitive unreleased lyrics.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow and endpoint allowlist describe API submission and downloads. This is declared behavior, not covert transfer.

content.value_statement: Music generation prompts often need structure, duration control, and clear model settings. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create RunComfy CLI requests for ElevenLabs Music.
actual_capabilities: Builds RunComfy CLI commands for the ElevenLabs Music generation model.
Helps structure prompts with genre, mood, tempo, instruments, vocals, and lyrics.
Supports full vocal songs, instrumental beds, short jingles, podcast intros, and game loops.
Documents duration control from 5 seconds to 5 minutes with music_length_ms.
Explains force_instrumental and output_format options for generated audio.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a valid RunComfy account or token.
Sends prompts and lyrics to RunComfy-hosted model infrastructure.
Does not verify copyright ownership for user-provided lyrics.
Does not expose voice cloning, text-to-speech, or sound-effects endpoints.

use_cases.title: Create a Video Theme Track
use_cases.description: Generate a structured vocal or instrumental track that matches a video length, mood, and section plan.

use_cases.title: Draft Brand Audio Assets
use_cases.description: Produce short jingles, stingers, podcast intros, and outros with clear duration and instrumentation settings.

use_cases.title: Prototype Game Background Loops
use_cases.description: Create loop-friendly instrumental beds with consistent tempo, groove, and mood for gameplay scenes.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Short Jingle
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple brand audio cue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 10-second cheerful instrumental jingle for my brand. Use bright marimba, light drums, and a clean ending. Save it to ./out.

prompt_templates.title: Make an Instrumental Bed
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for background audio with duration control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 60-second instrumental lo-fi background track for a study video. Use warm keys, soft drums, no vocals, and a steady loop-friendly groove.

prompt_templates.title: Compose a Structured Song
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with lyrics and section markers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 90-second indie-pop song with female lead vocal. Include Intro, Verse, Chorus, Bridge, and Outro sections using the lyrics I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Production Draft Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for cost-aware iteration before final render.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 35-second draft first, then prepare the final 3-minute RunComfy command after I approve the direction. Keep genre, BPM, structure, and lyrics consistent.

output_examples.input: A 15-second podcast intro with upbeat synths and no vocals.
output_examples.output: A concise RunComfy music-generation request with force_instrumental enabled, a 15000 ms duration, and an output directory for the downloaded audio.

output_examples.input: A 60-second pop song with verse and chorus lyrics.
output_examples.output: A structured prompt that combines style, tempo, vocal direction, section markers, lyrics, and duration for ElevenLabs Music.

output_examples.input: A loop-friendly game background track for a calm forest scene.
output_examples.output: An instrumental music brief with loop guidance, consistent groove, natural textures, duration settings, and RunComfy execution steps.

best_practices: Start with a short draft before paying for a long final render.
Put genre, mood, BPM, instruments, vocals, and section markers in one clear prompt.
Use force_instrumental and also state no vocals when requesting instrumental music.
anti_patterns: Do not include secrets, private plans, or sensitive unreleased lyrics in prompts.
Do not mix contradictory genres or moods in the same generation request.
Do not use copyrighted lyrics unless you have the required rights.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates RunComfy CLI requests for ElevenLabs Music, including songs, instrumental tracks, jingles, intros, and loops.

faq.question: Does it require an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses the RunComfy CLI and sends generation requests to RunComfy-hosted model infrastructure.

faq.question: Can it make instrumental music only?
faq.answer: Yes. Use force_instrumental and include no vocals in the prompt for stronger guidance.

faq.question: How long can generated tracks be?
faq.answer: The documented duration range is 5 seconds to 5 minutes per call.

faq.question: Does it check lyric rights?
faq.answer: No. Users must confirm that they have the rights to any lyrics or source material they provide.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk notice because it runs a scoped CLI, uses network calls, and depends on token storage.
